meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: LRASM Clears Integration Milestone for Use on the B-1B
immediate.summary: The Air Force recently tested its shared project with the Navy, the Long-Range Anti-Ship Missile, on the B-1B Lancer in a key milestone event for integrating the crucial munition on its bombers for potential strikes on adversaries. The post LRASM Clears Integration Milestone for Use on the B-1B appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Lockheed Martin to purchase Ultra Maritime in $3.5B deal
immediate.summary: Lockheed Martin said that Ultra Maritime will fall under the company’s rotary and mission systems division upon closing.

immediate.title: Iridium folds Aireon aviation safety service into Rocket Lab-bound business
immediate.summary: Iridium Communications has completed its takeover of Aireon, bringing the aircraft-tracking venture fully in-house ahead of the satellite operator’s planned $8 billion sale to Rocket Lab. The post Iridium folds Aireon aviation safety service into Rocket Lab-bound business appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Watch SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launch 81 satellites early on July 7
immediate.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket will launch 81 payloads to orbit from California early Tuesday morning (July 7), and you can watch the action live.

immediate.title: Deep-Space Radar Construction Delayed, GAO Says
immediate.summary: The Space Force approved its first of three deep-space radars for early operational use last fall, but construction slow-downs at the site in Western Australia have caused delays to other parts of the program, according to a government watchdog. The post Deep-Space Radar Construction Delayed, GAO Says appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies
immediate.summary: As NASA prepares for a sustained human presence on the Moon, missions will increasingly require spacecraft that can navigate and communicate without a direct connection to Earth. NASA’s Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment, or CAPSTONE, validated and advanced these capabilities. immediate.title: Ondas makes $876M acquisition of DYZNE, latest in acquisition spree
immediate.summary: After buying eight companies in the last two years, Ondas execs say they will focus on integrating their portfolio of drones “from surface to stratosphere” by developing a new AI command system in partnership with Palantir.

immediate.title: NASA just found a planet 'hiding' in TESS spacecraft data, all thanks to Einstein
immediate.summary: NASA's exoplanet-hunting spacecraft TESS has a new method for detecting worlds beyond the solar system, and it is thanks to Einstein.

immediate.title: Army makes new tweaks to acquisition portfolios, realigns autonomy office
immediate.summary: The Army’s approach to acquisition portfolios, which is in flux after an overhaul in the fall, is to “keep what is working and change what is not,” a service spokesperson told Breaking Defense.

immediate.title: Black holes buried in mysterious 'little red dot' galaxies could blast cosmic ghosts at Earth
immediate.summary: Mysterious "little red dots" discovered in the early universe by the James Webb Space Telescope could harbor buried black holes that fire high-energy neutrinos through the cosmos.

immediate.title: Unidentified metal spheres found on Australian beach are 'debris from a foreign rocket body', space agency says
immediate.summary: The Australian Space Agency is warning residents of Queensland to be on the lookout after metallic-looking spheres, possibly space debris, appeared on a beach.

immediate.title: 'Acceleration without fuel:' Revolutionary superconducting thruster harnesses Earth's magnetic field in 1st orbital test
immediate.summary: A novel space thruster using superconducting magnets completed its first orbital tests. The technology could in the future propel spacecraft using just the power of the sun.
